# Copying blog pages as markdown in Hugo (2026-03-05 09:00Z)

_**User**_

I want to add a feature to this blog which allows me to Copy the whole page and mark down. I want to use Hugo to create the markdown files that are also served. We have to make sure we update the static web app config so that it picks up the MD files and serves them. Here's an overall review of the sort of feature that I want. It's basically just a button on every single blog post at the top that says copy as markdown and it copies it to the clipboard.

---

## Serving Pages as Markdown in Hugo

Hugo has a built-in **Output Formats** system that lets you render every page into multiple formats simultaneously (HTML, JSON, RSS, and anything custom). That's the mechanism to use.

### Step 1 — Define the custom output format in `hugo.toml`

```toml
[mediaTypes]
  [mediaTypes."text/markdown"]
    suffixes = ["md"]

[outputFormats]
  [outputFormats.Markdown]
    mediaType = "text/markdown"
    baseName  = "index"
    isPlainText = true
```

This tells Hugo to produce a `index.md` file alongside the usual `index.html` for any page that uses it.

### Step 2 — Enable it for pages

```toml
[outputs]
  page = ["HTML", "Markdown"]
```

This makes every single content page generate both `index.html` and `index.md`. After `hugo build`, you'll find `public/your-post/index.md` sitting right next to `public/your-post/index.html`, so the `.md` URL just works automatically.

### Step 3 — Create the Markdown template

Create `layouts/_default/single.md`:

```
# {{ .Title }}

{{ .RawContent }}
```

Using `.RawContent` ensures you get the original Markdown without any HTML rendering, providing the cleanest possible input for an AI.

You can also add frontmatter fields if useful:

```
# {{ .Title }}

> {{ .Description }}
> Published: {{ .Date.Format "2006-01-02" }}

{{ .RawContent }}
```

### Step 4 — Add a "Copy page" button to your layout

In your single-page HTML template (e.g. `layouts/_default/single.html`), add a button that:
1. Fetches `./index.md` (relative to the current URL)
2. Copies the result to the clipboard

```html
<button id="copy-md-btn">Copy page</button>

<script>
document.getElementById('copy-md-btn').addEventListener('click', async () => {
  const mdUrl = window.location.pathname.replace(/\/?$/, '/') + 'index.md';
  const res  = await fetch(mdUrl);
  const text = await res.text();
  await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text);
  document.getElementById('copy-md-btn').textContent = 'Copied!';
  setTimeout(() => document.getElementById('copy-md-btn').textContent = 'Copy page', 2000);
});
</script>
```

This is exactly the same pattern Mintlify uses under the hood — async fetch of the `.md` URL, then write to clipboard. (Note: as discussed earlier, Safari can be finicky with async clipboard writes — see below for a workaround if you need it.)

---

### Bonus: `llms.txt` for the whole site

You can also generate an `llms.txt` at the site root that acts as a site map for AI agents, linking to the `.md` version of each page.

Add to `hugo.toml`:

```toml
[mediaTypes."text/plain"]
  suffixes = ["txt"]

[outputFormats.LLMS]
  mediaType = "text/plain"
  baseName  = "llms"
  isPlainText = true

[outputs]
  home = ["HTML", "LLMS"]
  page = ["HTML", "Markdown"]
```

Create `layouts/index.llms`:

```
# {{ .Site.Title }}

## Pages

{{- range .Site.RegularPages }}
- [{{ .Title }}]({{ .RelPermalink }}index.md): {{ .Summary | plainify }}
{{- end }}
```

After building, `public/llms.txt` will list every page with a direct link to its clean `.md` version.

---

### Safari clipboard fix

If you need Safari support, you can work around its synchronous clipboard restriction by pre-fetching the Markdown before the click event completes:

```js
// Pre-fetch on hover/focus so the data is ready synchronously on click
let cachedMd = null;
const btn = document.getElementById('copy-md-btn');
const mdUrl = window.location.pathname.replace(/\/?$/, '/') + 'index.md';

btn.addEventListener('mouseenter', async () => {
  if (!cachedMd) cachedMd = await fetch(mdUrl).then(r => r.text());
});

btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  if (cachedMd) navigator.clipboard.writeText(cachedMd);
});
```

Writing the copy button script... Writing the templates...

Now I'm styling the copy button to match the dark theme with a pill-shaped design similar to the tag styling, then building out the JavaScript to handle pre-fetching the markdown on hover and copying it to the clipboard on click with a "Copied!" confirmation message.

Writing the copy functionality...

Actually, I'm realizing Safari has a specific constraint here—`navigator.clipboard.writeText()` needs to be called synchronously within the event handler, so fetching asynchronously won't work. The prefetch on hover solves this elegantly since the markdown is already cached by the time the user clicks, letting me call `writeText` in the click handler's synchronous context. But I need to handle the case where someone clicks without hovering first, like on touch devices.

I should prefetch on `touchstart` for mobile and `mouseenter`/`focus` for desktop, then on click, use the cached version if available or fall back to an async fetch. Modern Safari (16.4+) handles async clipboard operations better anyway, so this pragmatic approach should work across browsers. For the template, I'll use `.RawContent` to render the markdown.

Now I'm ready to write out all the files.</details></think>

Everything is working. Here's a summary of what was done:

**7 files changed/created:**

1. **`hugo.toml`** - Added `text/markdown` media type, `Markdown` output format, and `LLMS` output format. Pages now output both HTML and Markdown; the home page additionally outputs `llms.txt`.

2. **`themes/stepdev/layouts/_default/single.md`** (new) - Markdown template using `.RawContent` so the output is clean, original Markdown with title, description, and date header.

3. **`themes/stepdev/static/js/copy-markdown.js`** (new) - External JS file (required because the CSP has `script-src 'self'`). Pre-fetches the `.md` file on hover/focus/touchstart for Safari compatibility, then copies to clipboard on click with a "Copied!" confirmation state.

4. **`themes/stepdev/layouts/_default/single.html`** - Added the "Copy as Markdown" button inline in the post meta row (next to the reading time), plus the script tag to load the JS.

5. **`themes/stepdev/static/css/style.css`** - Added `.copy-md-btn` styles matching the dark theme. Subtle border button that highlights blue on hover and green when copied.

6. **`staticwebapp.config.json`** - Added `.md` and `.txt` to the navigation fallback exclude list (so Azure SWA serves them directly instead of rewriting to `index.html`), and registered their MIME types.

7. **`themes/stepdev/layouts/index.llms`** (new) - Generates `llms.txt` at the site root with links to the `.md` version of every page, acting as an AI-readable sitemap.
